_comment: This is a comprehensive reference configuration file. It includes UI settings and an example of every available component.

ui_settings._comment: This section stores the state of the main UI controls for complete reproducibility.

components._comment: Sine: A standard sine wave. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees).
Sine (Varying Freq): A sine wave that abruptly changes frequency. Parameters: amplitude, start_freq (Hz), end_freq (Hz), change_time (s).
Cosine: A standard cosine wave. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees).
Square: A square wave. Parameters: amplitude, frequency (Hz), phase (degrees), and duty_cycle (0.0 to 1.0).
Chirp: A signal with a linearly increasing frequency. Parameters: amplitude, start_freq (Hz), end_freq (Hz).
Gaussian Noise: Adds random noise. The seed makes the noise pattern reproducible.
Amplitude Jump: An instantaneous DC shift in the signal.
Impulse Noise: A single, sharp spike added to the signal.
Outlier: Replaces a single data point with an extreme value.
Bias: Adds a constant DC offset to the entire signal.
Drift: Adds a linearly increasing or decreasing trend.
Signal Dropout: Forces the signal to be zero for a duration.
Saturation: Clips the signal's amplitude if it exceeds a threshold.
Time Delay: Shifts the entire signal forward in time.
